records.label: T1.1  Hermitian circulant M(a, b) = a I + b C + bbar C^2 is Hermitian
records.detail: M.H - M = 0 symbolically.

records.label: T1.2  Spectrum (a + 2 b_re, a - b_re - sqrt(3) b_im, a - b_re + sqrt(3) b_im)
records.detail: Computed spectrum = {sqrt(3)/4 + 1/2, 1/2 - sqrt(3)/4, 2} matches eigenvals = {sqrt(3)/4 + 1/2, 1/2 - sqrt(3)/4, 2}.

records.label: T1.3  Path origin (t=0): spectrum = (1 + sqrt(2), 1 - 1/sqrt(2), 1 - 1/sqrt(2))
T1.4  Path endpoint (t=1): all eigenvalues stay positive
records.detail: min eigenvalue at t=1 ~ 0.040350

records.label: T1.5  Finite-dim eta(M(a, b)) at t=1 = 3 (sum of signs of eigenvalues)
records.detail: All three eigenvalues positive => eta_finite = +3 (mod 1 = 0).

records.label: T2.1  eta(D(a, b)) is constant in t (no spectral flow on physical path)
records.detail: eta_grid: min = 3.0, max = 3.0, constant value = 3

records.label: T2.2  eta(D_phys) - eta(D_0) = 0 (no continuous content on the path)
records.detail: Delta eta = 0.000000; the moduli path generates ZERO eta change.

records.label: T2.3  arg(b)(1) - arg(b)(0) = 2/9, but eta change = 0
records.detail: arg(b) varies by 2/9 while eta stays at 3; clear unit/structure mismatch.

records.label: T2.4  Finite-dim eta is integer; cannot supply a fractional radian in t
records.detail: eta(M) = 3 throughout the path; arg(b) varies in (0, 2/9). Mismatch.

records.label: T2.5  Retained eta = 2/9 is equivariant fixed-point eta, not family eta
records.detail: eta(Z_3, (1,2)) = 2/9 from APS at fixed point; the FAMILY eta is the integer +3 everywhere on the moduli path.

records.label: T3.1  Spectral flow on physical moduli path = 0
records.detail: All three eigenvalues stay positive on the (a, |b|) = (1, 1/sqrt(2)) path.

records.label: T3.2  Path with a varying produces non-zero spectral flow (a -> 0 sweep)
records.detail: Found 1 zero crossings on s in [0, 1.5] at arg(b) = 2/9.

records.label: T3.3  Spectral-flow integer is +/- 1 (or higher integer), not 2/9
records.detail: SF = 1 (integer). To get fractional 2/9, would need SF + (continuous-part) = 2/9 with integer SF — impossible without rational continuous part.

records.label: T3.4  Continuous-part endpoint integral does not equal 2/9 in radians
T3.5  Closed-path eta change = SF (integer); pure rational 2/9 not reachable
records.detail: On a closed family path, eta jumps are integers. 2/9 requires open-path data.

records.label: T4.1  Convention (a) eta_diff = arg(b) literal: 0 =? 2/9
records.detail: observed = 0, target = 2/9. Mismatch by 2/9.

records.label: T4.2  Convention (b) eta_diff = arg(b) / (2 pi): 0 =? 1/(9 pi)
records.detail: target = 1/(9*pi); mismatch.

records.label: T4.3  Convention (c) eta_diff = arg(b) * (2 pi): 0 =? 4 pi / 9
records.detail: target = 4*pi/9; mismatch.

records.label: T4.4  Retained eta = 2/9 is NOT a family-eta difference (category error)
records.detail: eta(Z_3, (1, 2)) lives at a single fixed point; arg(b) is a moduli coordinate.

records.label: T4.5  Bridging mod-Z to mod-2pi requires same postulate P (no progress)
records.detail:
eta lives mod Z (unit = 1); arg(b) lives mod 2 pi. Bridging the two units
is exactly the unit conversion that postulate P names.

records.label: T4.6  Naive identification (mod Z = mod 2pi) circular; via-2pi gives 4 pi / 9
records.detail:
Either we postulate 'mod Z = mod 2 pi' (= postulate P), or use 2 pi factor
and get 4 pi / 9 != 2/9 (back to the radian-bridge no-go).

records.label: T5.1  CS integral mod Z = eta = 2/9 (Bismut-Cheeger)
records.detail: CS = 2/9 (mod Z, dimensionless rational).

records.label: T5.2  Phase from CS integral: exp(2 pi i * 2/9) has arg = 4 pi / 9
records.detail: phase arg = 4*pi/9; rational * pi as in radian-bridge no-go.

records.label: T5.3  Identifying phase arg with arg(b) requires pi = 1/2 (impossible)
records.detail: pi is not 1/2 in any retained framework. CS phase != literal arg(b).

records.label: T5.4  Wess-Zumino term WZ = 2 pi * eta = 4 pi / 9 (rational * pi)
records.detail: WZ = 4*pi/9; rational * pi structure.

records.label: T5.5  Bismut-Cheeger formula does NOT strip the 2 pi unit factor
records.detail:
BC: eta = CS - integer (mod Z). exp(2 pi i CS) = phase. The 2 pi is
intrinsic to the phase exponentiation. Stripping = postulate P.

records.label: T6.1  R1 (Free Dirac, M = 0): eta_diff = +3, target 2/9: NO match
records.detail: eta_phys - eta(M=0) = 3; target arg(b) = 2/9; mismatch.

records.label: T6.2  R2 (Uniform-spectrum, b = 0): eta_diff = 0, target 2/9: NO match
records.detail: eta_phys - eta_uniform = 0; target = 2/9; mismatch.

records.label: T6.3  R3 (Trivial cone-point): eta_diff = 0; same as R2
records.detail: Same value as R2; mismatch with target 2/9.

records.label: T6.4  R4 (kappa = 1 boundary): eta_diff = 1; target 2/9: NO match
records.detail: eta_phys - eta_kappa1 = 1; target = 2/9; mismatch.

records.label: T6.5  R5 (Berry m_0, delta = 0): eta_diff = 0; target 2/9: NO match
records.detail: eta_phys - eta_m0 = 0; target = 2/9; mismatch.

records.label: T6.6  All five reference points give INTEGER eta differences (not 2/9)
records.detail: Differences = [0, 1, 3]; 2/9 is not an integer.

records.label: T6.7  Retained eta = 2/9 is orbifold eta, not finite-dim signature
records.detail:
eta(Z_3, (1, 2)) at the orbifold fixed point is the equivariant index,
computed via Lefschetz / character data. It is NOT a signature of a
concrete Hermitian matrix.

records.label: T7.F1  eta lives mod Z; arg(b) lives mod 2 pi; unit mismatch unbridged
records.detail: Bridging requires explicit conversion; same as postulate P.

records.label: T7.F2  'mod Z to radian' lift = restated postulate P (no derivation)
records.detail: Saying 'eta = 2/9 in radians' instead of 'eta = 2/9 mod Z' is a relabel.

records.label: T7.F3  Spectral flow contributes only integer jumps to eta
records.detail: SF in Z; cannot supply 2/9 as a continuous fractional content.

records.label: T7.F4  All natural D_0 choices give INTEGER eta differences
records.detail: Five reference points tested; differences in {0, 1, 3}; never 2/9.

records.label: T7.F5  eta -> phase requires exp(2 pi i eta), which restores 2 pi factor
records.detail: phase = exp(2 pi i * 2/9) has arg = 4 pi / 9 != 2/9.

records.label: T7.F6  Retained eta = 2/9 is DIMENSIONLESS (mod Z), not radian
records.detail:
Computed via 8 routes (HZ signature, APS Dirac, Dedekind, ABSS, ...);
all give a pure rational, not a radian.

records.label: T7.F7  Family-index pushforward is K-theoretic, hence in K-group
records.detail:
Pushforward of eta class lives in K^*(moduli, Z) or rational tensor;
no radian unit emerges.

records.label: T7.F8  No 'hidden radian unit' inside spectral-flow / family-index machinery
records.detail:
All inputs are topological / K-theoretic; outputs inherit the same
structure. Radian unit is not generated by the machinery itself.

records.label: T8.1  eta-to-radian lift hypothesis closes radian-bridge P: NO
records.detail:
All natural reference points and normalizations fail to convert
eta = 2/9 (mod Z) to arg(b) = 2/9 (rad) without the postulate P bridge.

records.label: T8.2  Obstruction: spectral-flow content is integer; finite-dim eta is integer
records.detail: Family-eta diff is integer; cannot supply fractional 2/9 content.

records.label: T8.3  Restating eta = 2/9 (mod Z) as 'eta = 2/9 in radians' is a relabel
records.detail: It is the same number with a different unit label; no bridge derived.

records.label: T8.4  Bismut-Cheeger / WZ phase = 4 pi / 9 (rational * pi), not 2/9
records.detail: exp(2 pi i * eta) restores the 2 pi factor; same as no-go note.

records.label: T8.5  All natural reference operators give integer eta differences
records.detail: {0, 1, 3} on five candidates; never matches 2/9.

records.label: T8.6  Lift requires a structural new input not retained on present stack
records.detail:
Same as candidates (a),(b),(c) of KOIDE_Z3_QUBIT_RADIAN_BRIDGE_NO_GO_NOTE:
lattice radian quantum, 4x4 Wilson, or Z_3 d^2-power quantization.

tasks_tested: Family of effective Dirac operators D(a, b) on Yukawa moduli
eta(D(a, b)) along the natural moduli path
Spectral flow content (physical and a-varying paths)
Identification with arg(b) under three normalizations
obstruction_class: category-error: orbifold fixed-point eta vs family-eta + unit mismatch (mod Z vs mod 2 pi)
key_failure: All natural references give INTEGER eta differences {0, 1, 3}; Bismut-Cheeger phase has arg 4 pi / 9 (rational * pi), not 2/9 (pure rational).
minimal_extra_inputs_required: Z_3-orbit Wilson-line d^2-power quantization (no-go note candidate (c))
field-redefinition convention 'turns' instead of 'radians' for delta
